input.background: Our illustrative data comes from a health survey done in adolescents. The illustrative data set includes information on 654 individuals between $3-19$ years of age. Table 15.1 is a code book for the variables in the data set. The response variable is a measure of respiratory function called forced expiratory volume (variable name FEV) that measures respiratory capacity in liters/second units. High values represent high respiratory capacity.
input.question: We will consider the effects of two explanatory variables: SMOKE (coded 0 for ...

AGE.description: Age of the individuals in years.

FEV.description: Forced expiratory volume in liters/second, a measure of respiratory capacity.

HEIGHT.description: Height of the individuals in inches.

SEX.description: Gender of the individuals, coded as 0 for female and 1 for male.

SMOKE.description: Smoking status of the individuals, coded as 0 for no and 1 for yes.
